bio: I work on interferometric optical systems and physics-constrained inverse models, taking broadband measurements and interpreting them into physical quantities, at the Wellman Center for Photomedicine (Harvard Medical School). Historically, my work has included solid-state lasers and entangled-photon experiments and spectroscopic OCT. More recently, I'm increasingly focused on quantum information broadly including quantum optics and quantum networks, spin-based quantum nodes, photonic interconnects, entanglement distribution, and quantum photonic integrated circuits. Applying to physics PhD programs for Fall 2027. Outside the lab I travel, play neoclassical piano, immerse myself in other cultures and learn languages, and I am also an active caver.
currentlyText: finishing a laser safety calculator for tissue exposure limits and reading about topology and differential geometry.
researchInterests: I'm increasingly focused on quantum optics and quantum information including the physics of spin-based quantum nodes, photonic interconnects, and distributing entanglement between them, as well as many-body quantum systems and statistical physics. I’m also interested in theoretical physics (particle physics, QED, and QCD), deep learning, and laser physics, which is where I started.
